_about: PR5 素材:动作库中英双语内容包。21 个动作的英文名称与说明,供 Claude Code 在 v2 PR5 合并进 src/data/exerciseLibrary.ts(中文已在仓库内)。wgerImageCandidate = 我对'wger / 开源库是否可能有可用图片'的估计,供 Claude Code 抓图时参考。教练角色出品,内容保守、符合设计文档 §2 AS 安全约束。

steps_en: Hold a dumbbell or kettlebell against your chest with both hands; feet slightly wider than shoulders, toes turned slightly out
Inhale and sit back and down by bending the hips and knees; knees track over the toes
Descend until the thighs are near parallel, or to a depth that feels comfortable, keeping the torso upright
Exhale and drive up through the whole foot to stand
cues_en: Weight on the whole foot; don't let the knees cave in
Keep the torso tall; don't round or arch the back
mistakes_en: Rounding or arching the back while descending
goblet-squat.asSafety_en: Use light-to-moderate load with a neutral torso; avoid heavy axial loading of the spine. Stop on any sharp pain.
goblet-squat.alternative_en: Drop the weight for a bodyweight squat, or sit to a chair and stand back up (box squat) to reduce difficulty.

steps_en: Feet slightly wider than shoulders; raise both arms forward for balance
Sit back and down by bending the hips and knees; knees track over the toes
Descend to a comfortable depth with a neutral torso
Drive up through the whole foot, squeezing the glutes at the top
cues_en: Whole-foot contact; knees don't cave in
Squeeze the glutes to stand; don't use the lower back
mistakes_en: Rounding or arching the lower back
bw-squat.asSafety_en: Bodyweight only, neutral torso. Stop on any sharp pain.
bw-squat.alternative_en: Sit to a chair and stand back up (box squat) to reduce difficulty.

steps_en: Place one hand and one knee on a bench or stable surface; keep the back flat
Let the other hand hang naturally holding a dumbbell
Retract the shoulder blade first, then pull the dumbbell back and up toward your side, elbow close to the body
Lower under control; don't shrug
cues_en: Flat back; don't rotate the torso
Drive with the back, not by shrugging
mistakes_en: Twisting the torso to cheat the weight up
db-row.asSafety_en: The supported position protects the lower back; keep sets and reps equal on both sides. Stop on any sharp pain.
db-row.alternative_en: With no equipment, replace this 'pull' with the prone Y-T-W raise.

ytw.target_en: Upper back / posterior chain / rotator cuff (posture)
steps_en: Lie face down, forehead resting lightly on the mat, neck relaxed
Move the arms through three shapes in turn — Y, T, W — thumbs pointing up
In each shape, retract the shoulder blades and lift the arms slightly off the floor
Move slowly and under control; no swinging
cues_en: Drive from the upper back; don't shrug
Keep the neck relaxed, eyes down
mistakes_en: Over-extending the lower back to compensate
ytw.asSafety_en: Keep the range gentle; don't push through back pain. Stop on any sharp pain.
ytw.alternative_en: Standing wall angel as a substitute.

pushup.name_zh: 俯卧撑(上斜 / 跪姿 / 标准)
steps_en: Pick a level by difficulty: hands on a wall, then incline (hands on a table edge), then kneeling, then standard
Hands slightly wider than shoulders; body in one straight line
Bend the elbows and lower to a comfortable range
Press back up to the start
cues_en: Brace the core; don't let the hips sag
Elbows about 45 degrees to the torso
mistakes_en: Sagging the lower back / hips piking up
pushup.asSafety_en: Progress from easy to hard step by step; keep the core stable, no sagging. Stop on any sharp pain.
pushup.alternative_en: If strength is limited, start with the incline or kneeling version.

steps_en: Lie on your back, knees bent, feet hip-width on the floor
Squeeze the glutes and lift the hips until shoulder, hip, and knee form a straight line
Hold about 1 second at the top
Lower under control
cues_en: Drive with the glutes, not the lower back
Ribs down, core braced
mistakes_en: Pushing up with the lower back (over-extension)
Feet too far from the body
glute-bridge.asSafety_en: Anti-extension focus; don't drive with the lower back. Stop on any sharp pain.
glute-bridge.alternative_en: Reduce the range to regress, or single-leg glute bridge to progress.

steps_en: Lie on your back, arms pointing to the ceiling, hips and knees bent to 90 degrees
Press the lower back flat into the floor
Slowly extend the opposite arm and leg toward the floor
Return, then switch sides
cues_en: Keep the lower back flat on the floor throughout
Move slowly and keep breathing
mistakes_en: Lower back arching off the floor
dead-bug.asSafety_en: Keep the back flat, no spinal extension. Stop on any sharp pain.
dead-bug.alternative_en: Move only the legs (not the arms) to reduce difficulty.

steps_en: Stand in a split stance, back foot up on the toes
Lower straight down, back knee toward the floor
Drive up through the front heel
Keep sets and reps equal on both sides
cues_en: Keep the torso upright
Front knee tracks over the toes
mistakes_en: Leaning forward and rounding the back
split-squat.asSafety_en: Bodyweight or light-to-moderate load, equal on both sides (mind the scoliosis). Stop on any sharp pain.
split-squat.alternative_en: Hold a chair or wall for balance, or reduce the range.

steps_en: Lie on the floor holding dumbbells, elbows bent, upper arms resting on the floor
Press up until the arms are straight
Lower under control until the elbows lightly touch the floor
Repeat
cues_en: The floor limits shoulder over-extension — easier on the shoulders
Keep the wrists neutral
floor-press.asSafety_en: The floor limits shoulder extension, safer for beginners and for AS. Stop on any sharp pain.
floor-press.alternative_en: With no equipment, replace this 'press' with the push-up.

steps_en: Lie face down, arms extended forward
Gently lift the arms, chest, and legs slightly off the floor
Hold 1 to 2 seconds
Lower slowly
cues_en: Small, gentle range; neck neutral
Glutes and back work together
mistakes_en: Over-extending and cranking the lower back
superman.asSafety_en: Keep the range gentle; don't over-extend or push into pain. Stop on any sharp pain.
superman.alternative_en: Switch to the prone Y-T-W raise or bird dog.

steps_en: On all fours, back flat
Extend the opposite arm and leg until parallel with the torso
Hold 1 to 2 seconds, keeping the pelvis stable
Return, then switch sides
cues_en: Pelvis doesn't rotate or tilt
Neck and spine stay neutral
bird-dog.asSafety_en: Anti-rotation, slow and controlled, spine neutral. Stop on any sharp pain.
bird-dog.alternative_en: Extend only the leg or only the arm to reduce difficulty.

steps_en: Lie on your side, forearm on the floor, elbow under the shoulder
Lift the hips so the body forms one straight line
Hold for the set time
Equal on both sides
cues_en: Body in one straight line
Hips don't sag
side-plank.asSafety_en: Keep sets and reps equal on both sides; mind the scoliosis symmetry. Stop on any sharp pain.
side-plank.alternative_en: Bent-knee side plank (knees down) to reduce difficulty.

steps_en: On all fours
Inhale and gently drop the belly, lift the head (cow)
Exhale and gently round the back, tuck the head (cat)
Alternate slowly within a comfortable range
cues_en: Sync with the breath
Gentle range; don't go to the painful end
mistakes_en: Too large a range, pushing into pain
cat-cow.asSafety_en: Gentle mobility work; don't go to the painful end. Stop on any sharp pain.
cat-cow.alternative_en: Do gentle spinal flexion and extension seated or standing.

steps_en: On all fours, or lying on your side
Place one hand behind the head or reach it upward
Rotate the upper back open, eyes following the hand
Return slowly; equal on both sides
cues_en: Rotation comes from the thoracic spine, not the lower back
Keep the pelvis stable
t-rotation.asSafety_en: Rotation from the thoracic spine, pelvis stable, equal on both sides. Stop on any sharp pain.

steps_en: Half-kneeling, push the hip of the back leg forward
Squeeze the glute and find a posterior pelvic tilt
Hold about 30 seconds
Equal on both sides
cues_en: Posterior pelvic tilt; don't arch and push the belly forward
Feel the stretch in the front of the hip
mistakes_en: Arching the back and pushing forward
hip-flexor-stretch.asSafety_en: Gentle static stretch, not into sharp pain. Stop on any sharp pain.

steps_en: Lying on your back or seated
Extend one leg and gently feel the stretch in the back of the thigh
Hinge from the hip as much as possible, keeping the spine neutral
Hold about 30 seconds; equal on both sides
cues_en: Keep the spine as neutral as possible
Gentle, no bouncing
mistakes_en: Rounding the back to force a reach for the toes
hamstring-stretch.asSafety_en: Avoid loading the spine in deep flexion; use a gentle static stretch. Stop on any sharp pain.
hamstring-stretch.alternative_en: Lying down, loop a towel around the foot to stretch with less forward bending.

chest-opener.target_en: Pecs / front of the shoulder (counters rounded shoulders)
steps_en: Stand beside a doorway or wall corner, forearm against the frame
Gently lean the body forward, opening up the chest
Hold about 30 seconds
Equal on both sides
cues_en: Feel the stretch across the front of the chest
Don't shrug
mistakes_en: Too large a range, stretching into shoulder pain
chest-opener.asSafety_en: Gentle stretch to counter a rounded posture; not into sharp pain. Stop on any sharp pain.
chest-opener.alternative_en: Lie on a foam roller for a thoracic extension stretch.

pelvic-clock.target_en: Awareness and mobility of the pelvis / lower back
steps_en: Lie on your back, knees bent
Imagine the pelvis is a clock; gently move it forward and back (12 to 6 o'clock)
Then gently side to side (3 to 9 o'clock)
Slowly trace small circles
cues_en: Small, slow, with awareness
Relax the breathing
pelvic-clock.asSafety_en: Small, gentle mobility work. Stop on any sharp pain.
pelvic-clock.alternative_en: Do only the forward-and-back gentle pelvic tilt.

gentle-extension.name_zh: 温和后伸(眼镜蛇 / 婴儿式交替)
steps_en: Lie face down, hands on the floor, gently lift the upper body to a comfortable range (cobra)
Hold a few seconds
Return to lying down, or move into child's pose to relax
Alternate gently
cues_en: Gentle range; neck neutral
Don't go to the painful end
gentle-extension.asSafety_en: Extension is a key direction for AS, but keep it gentle and pain-free; if night pain or sharp pain appears, stop and see a doctor.
gentle-extension.alternative_en: Standing, hands on the lower back, do a smaller-range gentle extension.

steps_en: Seated or lying down, place both hands on the sides of the ribs
Inhale deeply through the nose, feeling the rib cage expand to the sides
Exhale slowly
Repeat
cues_en: Breathe into the rib cage, not by shrugging
Relaxed rhythm
mistakes_en: Over-breathing to the point of dizziness
breathing.asSafety_en: Gentle breathing work; helps rib-cage mobility for AS. Stop if uncomfortable.
breathing.alternative_en: Lie with knees bent and do belly / rib breathing.

steps_en: Choose flat ground
Head up, chin tucked, arms swinging naturally
Pace so you can still talk normally
Continue for the set time
cues_en: Tall posture, no slouching
Land softly
mistakes_en: Walking fast with the head down and chest collapsed
Pace too hard to catch your breath
walk.asSafety_en: Low-impact cardio; avoid high-impact running and jumping. Stop on discomfort or sharp pain.
walk.alternative_en: March in place indoors, or shorten the time.
